article_title: Extension to direct delivery from off-site storage
body: Issue date: May 30, 2023 This change applies to the following licences: The Liquor and Cannabis Regulation Branch amended the Manufacturer licence terms and conditions to extend the authorization for B.C. manufacturers to deliver directly to retail customers from their on-site store or registered off-site storage facilities up to and includingJune 30, 2025. Manufacturers can temporarily deliver directly to retail customers using their own employees or a third-party delivery service. This temporary measure does not authorize liquor manufacturers to sell liquor from their off-site storage locations. Customers will still be required to complete transactions through a licensee’s on-site store, online, or by telephone – they will not be able to physically purchase products at off-site storage locations. The updated terms and conditions and more information can be found in theManufacturer Licence Handbook [627KB,PDF]. Bulletin 23-05 replacespolicy directive 22-09and the previous expiry date of June 30, 2023. Bulletins are for general information and may not apply to all situations. Bulletins do not constitute legal advice nor are they a comprehensive statement of the legal obligations that arise under the Liquor Control and Licensing Act, Cannabis Control and Licensing Act, regulations, or any other applicable laws. When interpreting and applying the information contained in the Bulletins, you are encouraged to seek advice from your professional advisors as appropriate in the circumstances. The B.C. Public Service acknowledges the territories of First Nations around B.C. and is grateful to carry out our work on these lands. We acknowledge the rights, interests, priorities, and concerns of all Indigenous Peoples - First Nations, Métis, and Inuit - respecting and acknowledging their distinct cultures, histories, rights, laws, and governments.

minutes_briefs.title: BC liquor manufacturers can deliver directly to customers until June 30, 2025
minutes_briefs.summary: The BC government has extended a temporary rule that lets liquor manufacturers deliver directly to retail customers from their on-site store or registered off-site storage. This extension runs until June 30, 2025. Manufacturers can use their own employees or a third-party delivery service, but customers must still order through the manufacturer's on-site store, online, or by phone — they cannot buy products in person at off-site storage locations.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If you are a BC liquor manufacturer, update your delivery operations to comply with the extended deadline of June 30, 2025.

key_dates.label: Direct delivery authorization for BC liquor manufacturers expires
